description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with a prominent central chimney and a mix of stone, timber, and plaster on its exterior. The front facade displays a covered porch with wooden supports and a large arched window beneath the roofline, while the lower level is constructed from stone blocks. Timber framing and decorative cross-bracing are visible on the side elevations, giving the structure a medieval or Tudor Revival appearance. The windows are small and irregularly placed, with some grouped in pairs and others standing alone. The rear of the house is dominated by the chimney, which extends from the ground to above the roofline, and the overall structure is narrow and vertically oriented. The color palette consists of muted browns, beiges, and blacks, contributing to its historic and rustic character.
description: A narrow, vertically oriented house with a steep gable roof, central chimney, timber framing, and stone base, featuring a covered porch and arched window on the front facade.
description_view_0: Front view shows the covered porch, arched window, and main entrance with timber and stone details.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the steep gable roof, decorative timber cross-bracing, and the tall chimney.
description_view_2: Rear view is dominated by the large chimney running up the center, with small paired windows and stone base visible.
description_view_3: Right side view displays more timber framing, small windows, and the continuation of the stone foundation.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with wooden supports, arched window above, and stone steps leading to a single main door.
